case_title: RIVER GARDEN RETIREMENT HOME VS. CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD AN AGENCY OF THE

REPLY TO PL'S OPPOSITION TO DF'S DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S (AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. MATTER WAS ARGUED. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE SECTION 24402 IS UNCONSTITUTIONAL AND NOT SUBJECT TO REFORMATION TO EXPAND THE SCOPE OF ITS DEDUCTION. AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2, THE COURT OVERRULED THE DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, COURT TRIAL SET FOR SEP-22-2008 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. NOTICE OF MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE ON SEP-05-2008 AT 2:30 PM IN DEPARTMENT 504. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & PLAINTIFF RIVER GARDEN RETIREMENT HOME'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT- DEFENDANTS MOTION IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE EVEN IF PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF DUE AND PAYABLE WERE ACCEPTED, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PAY WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE AND DEMAND. THEREFORE THE COURT NEED NOT REACH THE OTHER ISSUES RAISED. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER TO REFLECT THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING WHICH NOW BECOMES FINAL AND PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & PLAINTIFF RIVER GARDEN RETIREMENT HOME'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT- DEFENDANTS MOTION IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE EVEN IF PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF DUE AND PAYABLE WERE ACCEPTED, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PAY WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE AND DEMAND. THEREFORE THE COURT NEED NOT REACH THE OTHER ISSUES RAISED. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER TO REFLECT THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING WHICH NOW BECOMES FINAL AND PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G). DAVID LEW, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, (510)622-2225, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY
AMY L. SILVERSTEIN, ESQ. AND EDWIN P. ANTOLIN, ESQ.(SILVERSTEIN & POMERANTZ, LLP), (415)593-3502 AND (415)593-3505, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S(AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE SECTION 24402 IS UNCONSTITUTIONAL AND NOT SUBJECT TO REFORMATION TO EXPAND THE SCOPE OF ITS DEDUCTION. AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2, THE COURT OVERRULED THE DEMURRER. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB/AL)
